input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: Now I'll set up BenchmarkDotNet to compare .NET 9 vs .NET 10. I need to multi-target the library and create a benchmark project.
data.reasoningText:
Good, I have the source code. Now I need to create a BenchmarkDotNet project that benchmarks the three methods (`AverageWordLength`, `WordLengthHistogram`, `UniqueWordCount`) across both .NET 9 and .NET 10. I'll use BenchmarkDotNet with multi-TFM targeting.

Let me create a benchmark project that:
1. References the TextAnalyzer project
2. Targets both net9.0 and net10.0
3. Benchmarks the three methods

Since the TextAnalyzer project currently targets net9.0, I'll need to update it to support both, or I can just make the benchmark project include the source directly. 

I'll start by updating the TextAnalyzer project to multi-target both frameworks, then create a benchmark project that references it and also targets both net9.0 and net10.0.

data.content: I need to configure BenchmarkDotNet to explicitly target both runtimes. Let me update the benchmark code.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet multi-TFM requires the host to run on one framework, but BDN itself can launch child processes targeting different runtimes. Let me run it from one framework (net10.0, the latest SDK) and configure BDN to benchmark both runtimes using Job attributes.

I need to update the benchmark code to use `[SimpleJob(RuntimeMoniker.Net90)]` and `[SimpleJob(RuntimeMoniker.Net100)]` attributes instead of the generic `[SimpleJob]`.

data.reasoningText:
The BenchmarkDotNet version might not have `Net100` yet. Let me check the available RuntimeMoniker values or use a different approach. Let me check which version of BenchmarkDotNet we're using and what monikers are available.

Actually, for .NET 10 the moniker might be `Net10_0` or something different. Let me check.

Actually, looking at the BenchmarkDotNet source, .NET 10 support was added relatively recently. The moniker might be `Net100` in newer versions. Let me check what version of BenchmarkDotNet was resolved.

Actually, the error says it only fails for net9.0 target. 

The net9.0 build is probably pulling in an older version of BenchmarkDotNet that doesn't support `Net100` yet. Instead of relying on the predefined moniker, I could try creating a custom runtime configuration using `CoreRuntime.CreateForNewVersion` to manually specify the .NET 10 runtime.
